text.title: Stakeholder Analysis — Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every application of Stakeholder Analysis must enforce.

rule.statement: Every stakeholder row names an individual (handle, email, or named role with rotation). 'The marketing team' is not a stakeholder.
rule.rationale: Anonymous teams cannot be engaged on a cadence; engagement plans only work against named humans.

rule.statement: Every stakeholder is classified on a 2×2 power × interest grid with explicit cell rationale.
rule.rationale: Uncalibrated engagement (everyone gets every email) burns stakeholder trust; per-quadrant strategy concentrates BA effort.

rule.statement: Each quadrant maps to a defined engagement strategy (manage closely / keep satisfied / keep informed / monitor).
rule.rationale: Without a per-quadrant strategy, stakeholders default to the loudest cadence, not the most appropriate one.

rule.statement: Every major decision in the engagement has a RACI row identifying R, A, C, I individuals.
rule.rationale: Decisions without an Accountable individual stall; decisions without Consulted individuals breed surprise dissent.

rule.statement: Communications plan names cadence + channel + named owner per stakeholder.
rule.rationale: Comms drift when cadence is undefined; channel drift produces missed messages.
